info.title: AnvilML API
info.description: AnvilML — GPU job orchestration server API

get.description:
Returns HTTP 200 with a JSON body containing the application status,
version, and uptime in seconds since start.
Returns a JSON array of `ArtifactMeta` objects sorted newest-first.

parameters.name: before
parameters.description: Only artifacts created before this Unix timestamp

get.summary: Get an artifact by its content hash
get.description:
Returns the raw PNG bytes with:
- `Content-Type: image/png`
- `Cache-Control: public, immutable, max-age=31536000`
- `ETag: "{hash}"`

Returns 404 JSON when the artifact file does not exist.

parameters.name: hash
parameters.description: SHA-256 hex digest of the artifact

get.description:
Upgrades the HTTP connection to a WebSocket, subscribes to the shared
[`EventBroadcaster`], and forwards each event as a JSON text frame.
Returns a JSON array of `Job` objects sorted newest-first.

parameters.name: before
parameters.description: Only jobs created before this ISO 8601 timestamp

post.summary: Submit a new job for execution
post.description:
Validates the graph structure via the scheduler, persists the job to SQLite,
enqueues it, and returns 202 with the real job_id and queue position.
Returns 503 `workers_unavailable` when the Python preflight check has failed
(unless `ANVILML_WORKER_MOCK` is set).

delete.description:
Deletes all jobs matching the given status filter (default: all terminal
jobs). For each matched job, artifacts are deleted first (best-effort),
then the job row is removed from the database.

* `status` — `completed|failed|cancelled|all` (case-insensitive).
  Defaults to `all` when omitted.

Returns `{ "removed": <count> }` with 200 OK.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Filter by status: completed, failed, cancelled, or all (default: all)

get.description: Returns 200 with the full `Job` record if found, or 404 if not found.

delete.summary: Delete a terminal job and its artifacts
delete.description:
Returns 204 No Content on success. Returns 404 if the job does not exist,
or 409 if the job is in a non-terminal state (Queued or Running).

The job must be in a terminal state (Completed, Failed, or Cancelled).
On success, all on-disk artifact files are removed first, then the job
and artifact rows are deleted from the database.

409.description: Job is not terminal — cannot delete

post.summary: Cancel a queued or running job
post.description:
Transitions the job to `Cancelled` and returns 202. Returns 404 if the job
does not exist, or 409 if the job is in a terminal state.

get.description:
Returns a JSON array of all scanned model metadata, optionally filtered
by `kind`. Delegates to `registry.list(kind)` from the application state.

post.description:
Triggers a background model directory rescan. The handler returns 202
Accepted immediately; the actual scanning work is performed by a spawned
tokio task.

get.description:
Returns the model metadata for a single model identified by its ID.
Returns 200 with the model on success, or 404 with an error JSON body
when no model with the given ID exists.
Returns the hardware detection result collected at server startup.

Returns the `EnvReport` populated by the Python preflight check at startup.
In test context (no preflight runs), stub values are returned.
Returns a JSON array of `WorkerInfo` objects, one per worker in the pool.

post.description: Sends a shutdown to the specified worker, force-kills it, and respawns it.

ArtifactMeta.description: Metadata about an artifact produced by a generation job.

id.description: Unique identifier for this artifact (UUID v4).

job_id.description: The job that produced this artifact.

model_id.description: The model that produced this artifact.

path.description: Filesystem path to the artifact file.

size_bytes.description: Size of the artifact file in bytes.

CapabilitySource.description: Source used to resolve inference capabilities for a GPU device.

ClearJobsResponse.description: Response body for the `DELETE /v1/jobs` bulk-clear endpoint.

EnumerationSource.description: Source used to enumerate a GPU device.

EnvReport.description: Python environment health report from the preflight check.

preflight_ok.description: Whether the preflight checks all passed.

reason.description: Reason if preflight failed; empty string if it succeeded.

ErrorInline.description: Error response body for graph validation failures.

GpuDevice.description: A single GPU device detected on the host.

arch.description: Architecture identifier — CUDA SM version ("8.6") or AMD gfx ("gfx1100").

capabilities_source.description: Source used to resolve inference capabilities.

caps.description: Inference capability flags resolved from the device database or defaults.

db_group_name.description:
Database group name — the model name from the device capability DB, shown in parentheses
alongside a preserved Vulkan SKU name (e.g. "Radeon RX 7900 XTX (Navi 31 XL)").

enumeration_source.description: Source used to enumerate this device.

index.description: Zero-based index of this GPU device.

pci_device_id.description: PCI device ID within the vendor.

pci_vendor_id.description: PCI vendor ID (e.g. 0x10DE for NVIDIA, 0x1002 for AMD).

vram_free_mib.description: Free VRAM in MiB (refreshed periodically from worker MemoryReport events).

GpuStatSnapshot.description: GPU VRAM snapshot for a single device at a point in time.

index.description: Zero-based index of the GPU device.

vram_total_mib.description: Total VRAM available on this GPU in MiB.

vram_used_mib.description: VRAM currently used by this GPU in MiB.

HardwareInfo.description: Complete hardware report for the host.

inference_caps.description: Inference capability flags derived from device type and driver.

uptime_s.description: Uptime in seconds since server start.

cpu_model.description: CPU model string (e.g. "Intel(R) Xeon(R) CPU E5-2686 v4 @ 2.30GHz").

InferenceCaps.description: Inference capability flags for detected hardware.

bf16.description: Whether the device supports BF16 (bfloat16) inference.

flash_attention.description: Whether the device supports Flash Attention.

fp16.description: Whether the device supports FP16 (half-precision) inference.

fp32.description: Whether the device supports FP32 (single-precision) inference.

fp4.description: Whether the device supports FP4 inference.

fp8.description: Whether the device supports FP8 inference.

nvfp4.description: Whether the device supports NVIDIA FP4 (NVFP4) inference.

Job.description: A generation job tracked by the scheduler.

artifact_count.description: Number of artifacts produced so far.

completed_at.description: When the job reached a terminal state. `None` while in-flight.

created_at.description: When the job was created (always set).

device_index.description: Assigned GPU device index. `None` until dispatched.

error.description: Error message if the job failed. `None` otherwise.

graph.description: The validated DAG graph (as raw JSON).

settings.description: Generation settings supplied at submission time.

worker_id.description: The worker that processed this job. `None` until dispatched.

JobCancelledEvent.description: Job-cancelled event — emitted when a user cancels a running job.

JobCompletedEvent.description: Job-completed event — emitted when a job finishes successfully.

JobFailedEvent.description: Job-failed event — emitted when a job encounters an unrecoverable error.

traceback.description: Optional stack trace or additional diagnostic context.

JobImageReadyEvent.description: Job-image-ready event — emitted when a worker produces an output image.

artifact_hash.description: SHA-256 hash used to fetch the artifact via `GET /v1/artifacts/:hash`.

job_id.description: UUID of the job that produced this image.

seed.description: Random seed used to generate this image.

JobProgressEvent.description:
Job-progress event — emitted periodically during job execution to report
DAG node completion progress.

job_id.description: UUID of the job being executed.

node_index.description: Index of the node that just completed (0-based).

node_total.description: Total number of nodes in the DAG.

node_type.description: Type / class name of the completed node.

step.description:
Per-step progress within a node. Reserved for future use; always `None`
in the MVP.

step_total.description:
Total steps within a node. Reserved for future use; always `None` in
the MVP.

timestamp.description: When this progress update was emitted.

JobQueuedEvent.description: Job-queued event — emitted when a new job enters the queue.

job_id.description: UUID of the newly queued job.

JobSettings.description: Generation parameters supplied by the frontend when creating a job.

seed.description:
Seed for the random number generator. `-1` means "random", resolved
by the worker at execution time.

JobStartedEvent.description: Job-started event — emitted when a worker begins executing a job.

job_id.description: UUID of the job that started.

JobStatus.description: Lifecycle status of a generation job.

ModelMeta.description: Metadata about a scanned model file.

dtype_hint.description: Hint about the data type of the model weights.

id.description: Unique identifier for the model (opaque string).

path.description: Filesystem path to the model weights.

scanned_at.description: When this model was last scanned by the registry.

size_bytes.description: Size of the model file in bytes.

graph.description: The validated DAG graph (as raw JSON).

job_id.description: The UUID of the newly created job.

SystemStatsEvent.description: System statistics event — periodically broadcast to report hardware state.

ram_used_mib.description: Host RAM currently used in MiB.

WorkerInfo.description: Information about a managed worker process.

current_job_id.description: The job currently being executed. `None` when idle/dead.

device_name.description: Name of the GPU device (or "CPU" for CPU workers).

vram_used_mib.description: VRAM consumed by the worker in MiB.

WorkerStatus.description: Lifecycle status of a worker process.

WorkerStatusChangedEvent.description:
Worker-status-changed event — emitted whenever a worker's lifecycle state
transitions (e.g. Idle → Busy, Busy → Dead).

status.description: The new lifecycle status of the worker.

oneOf.description: Job has been queued for execution.

JobQueued.description: Job has been queued for execution.

oneOf.description: Job execution has started on a worker.

JobStarted.description: Job execution has started on a worker.

oneOf.description: An output image is available for retrieval.

JobImageReady.description: An output image is available for retrieval.

oneOf.description: Job was cancelled by the user.

JobCancelled.description: Job was cancelled by the user.

WsEvent.description: All WebSocket event types that the server may broadcast to connected clients.
